danger_signals.visual_form: enemy sprites with aggressive animations in overworld and dungeon terrain
danger_signals.meaning: areas populated with combat threats requiring player engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: enclosed cave/fortress structures with distinct visual palette from overworld
danger_signals.meaning: high-danger zones containing bosses and powerful enemies

danger_signals.visual_form: large-scale enemy sprites, unique color schemes, dramatic positioning on screen
danger_signals.meaning: critical encounter points marking story progression and major threats

danger_signals.visual_form: bottom-screen gauge emptying during combat exchanges
danger_signals.meaning: character vulnerability window; danger of reduced damage output

danger_signals.visual_form: party member health gauge at bottom of screen depleting (red coloration implied by design era)
danger_signals.meaning: character proximity to defeat; need for healing or retreat

safety_signals.visual_form: populated settlements with NPCs, shops, distinct from wilderness
safety_signals.meaning: safe zones for rest, healing, equipment upgrades, story exposition

safety_signals.visual_form: non-hostile character sprites with neutral/welcoming animations
safety_signals.meaning: dialogue opportunities, quest markers, world-state information

safety_signals.visual_form: inns and rest areas within towns (implied visual distinction)
safety_signals.meaning: full restoration of hit points and magic points
weapon upgrade safety; progression through Weapon Orb investment

safety_signals.visual_form: Cannon Travel Centers with distinct landmark appearance
safety_signals.meaning: safe fast-travel between regions; strategic movement without combat

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: imperial soldiers/fortress guards (inferred from plot of 'empire conquering world with ancient flying fortress')
eight distinct elemental manifestations (water, earth, life, etc.)
player-controlled character sprites with distinct silhouettes and color differentiation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: towns allied with party, areas of active quest progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: spirits appear as boss-tier entities in dungeons with unique sprite designs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: spirits block progression; require defeat or negotiation before magic becomes available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ring Command menu expands to show spell lists for rescued elementals
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: party gains offensive/defensive/support spell capabilities; magic points deplete on cast

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Weapon Orbs appear as collectible items in dungeons and boss drops
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: blacksmith interactions enable weapon reforging; visual skill-level progression unlocks special attacks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: new regions unlock on overworld map; terrain and enemy types shift with geography
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPC dialogue changes; new elemental spirits become accessible; empire presence intensifies visually

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mode 7 effect demonstration (Flammie flying sequences) showing fortress as environmental threat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: overworld traversal becomes three-dimensional; final-stage enemy encounters intensify

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: three distinct player character sprites (hero, girl, sprite) with different color/design silhouettes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: each character occupies screen position; AI controls non-selected members; switchable control during pause

environmental_storytelling.example: fortress designs reflect imperial conquest aesthetic; spirit-guarded areas show elemental theming (water caves drip, fire dungeons glow)
town populations signal safety and civilization; isolated NPCs in wilderness suggest quest-givers or danger contexts
visual presence of upgradeable weapons in boss drops signals progression gates; blacksmith expertise ties to settlement development
overworld wilderness vs. enclosed dungeons communicate danger escalation; Mode 7 flying demonstrates scale of final threat (flying fortress)
eight elementals each have unique sprite and thematic environment; water spirit in water dungeon, etc., creates cohesive magical world-building

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: responds to directional input; highlighted in Ring Command menu; positioned at screen center for combat
follow selected character on-screen; customizable AI settings determine spell-casting and attack priorities; visible position indicates combat participation
animate toward party; trigger real-time combat; display health gauges; boss entities show unique attack patterns and larger sprite footprints
depletes on attack initiation; recharges over time; full bar indicates maximum damage potential; visual feedback on swinging weapons
occupy dungeons as boss encounters; possess unique combat patterns; block access to elemental magic
become available spell sources; Ring Command menu displays their magic; spells appear in combat rotation
stationary in town; triggers equipment upgrade dialog when approached; consumes Weapon Orbs to enhance weapons
launch party between regions; provide safe travel alternative to wilderness traversal; visually distinct landmark

notes: Secret of Mana communicates world-state primarily through environmental contrast (town vs. wilderness), character sprite differentiation, and UI manifestation (Ring Command menu expansion as magic/items unlock). Danger signals rely on creature presence and dungeon transitions rather than explicit visual warnings. Faction identity is weakly defined in source material; Empire faction inferred from plot summary. The power bar mechanic is core to combat danger communication—its visual state directly indicates offensive vulnerability. Elemental theming drives both narrative progression and environmental storytelling; each rescued spirit unlocks visually represented magic options. Mode 7 flying sequences serve as ultimate world-state marker (flying fortress threat materialization). Three-player cooperative system is communicated through on-screen character positioning and AI behavior visibility.
